An Economic Agent refers to a person or entity that makes decisions regarding consumption, investment and production in response to changes in prices within an economy (document: ECONOMY). These agents include consumers who decide what goods and services to buy based on their preferences and income levels; firms which determine the quantity of output to produce given market conditions like input costs and technology advancements, as well as consumer demand. Investors also fall under this category by determining where capital should be allocated for maximum return (document: ECONOMY).
